title: NADE Disability Examiner Position Paper (2004)
description: NADE's full position paper articulating the unique knowledge, skills, and abilities of the state DDS disability examiner who makes the initial medical-legal-vocational determination for SSA's SSDI and SSI programs. It argues the examiner must interchangeably apply the 'logic' of a physician, attorney, and rehabilitation counselor; details the sequential evaluation process, evidence weighing, childhood-SSI functional domains, and required technological proficiency; and cites SSA Advisory Board and GAO reports plus program statistics (e.g., 2003 DI/SSI benefit dollars and 2.5M initial determinations). Concludes that technology cannot replace the highly trained examiner. Approved by the NADE Board 2004-02-13. Scraped from the official NADE site.

source.title: NADE Board Approves Disability Examiner Position Paper

notes: Authoritative: direct capture of NADE's published Disability Examiner Position Paper (source URL preserved). temporal_coverage set to the board approval date (2004-02-13) explicitly stated at the end of the document; embedded program statistics reference 2003. Quotes the Social Security Advisory Board's 'The Social Security Definition of Disability' (Oct 2003) and GAO-04-121. Long-form narrative; no tabular data.
